message:
fix(install): Add lock ownership tracking to prevent cleanup race conditions

Introduces LOCK_OWNED flag to ensure install.sh only removes the lock file
it created, preventing a race condition where concurrent processes could
interfere with each other's locks.

Problem:
The previous implementation unconditionally removed /tmp/ubs-install.lock in
cleanup_on_exit(), even when the current process failed to acquire it. This
created a subtle race:

1. Process A acquires lock (creates /tmp/ubs-install.lock directory)
2. Process B tries to acquire lock, fails, calls cleanup_on_exit() on exit
3. Process B removes the lock directory that Process A is still using
4. Process A continues running thinking it has exclusive access
5. Process C can now acquire the "free" lock, causing concurrent installs

Impact:
This race was unlikely but could cause:
- Corrupted dependency downloads (two processes writing to same temp files)
- Incomplete installations (one process cleaning up while other still running)
- False "another instance running" errors (lock removed prematurely)

Solution:
- Add LOCK_OWNED=0 flag initialized at script start
- Set LOCK_OWNED=1 only after successfully creating lock directory
- In cleanup_on_exit(), only rmdir lock if LOCK_OWNED=1
- Processes that fail to acquire lock leave it alone during cleanup

This follows the principle: only clean up resources you successfully acquired.

Testing:
- Single instance: Works as before (acquires lock, cleans up on exit)
- Concurrent instances: Second instance exits cleanly without touching lock
- Crash scenarios: Lock owner cleans up; failed acquirer doesn't interfere

Related patterns:
This is analogous to file descriptor ownership in RAII - the process that
opens the FD is responsible for closing it, not processes that tried and
failed to open it.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,6 +46,8 @@ RUN_VERIFICATION=1
 TEMP_FILES=()
 # Lock file for concurrent execution prevention (MUST be fixed name, not $$)
 LOCK_FILE="/tmp/ubs-install.lock"
+# Track if we own the lock (only remove it if we created it)
+LOCK_OWNED=0
 
 cleanup_on_exit() {
   # Clean up temporary files
@@ -58,8 +60,10 @@ cleanup_on_exit() {
   rm -f /tmp/ast-grep-install.log /tmp/jq-install.log /tmp/ripgrep-install.log 2>/dev/null
   rm -f /tmp/download-error.log /tmp/sed-error.log /tmp/bug-scan.txt 2>/dev/null
   rm -rf /tmp/ripgrep.tar.gz /tmp/ripgrep-* /tmp/ast-grep.zip /tmp/ast-grep 2>/dev/null
-  # Remove lock file
-  rmdir "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null
+  # Remove lock file ONLY if we created it (don't remove another process's lock!)
+  if [ "$LOCK_OWNED" -eq 1 ]; then
+    rmdir "$LOCK_FILE" 2>/dev/null
+  fi
 }
 
 # Set up cleanup trap
@@ -1969,6 +1973,8 @@ main() {
     error "If this is incorrect, remove: $LOCK_FILE"
     exit 1
   fi
+  # Mark that we own the lock (so cleanup will remove it)
+  LOCK_OWNED=1
 
   # Save original arguments for potential re-exec during update
   local ORIGINAL_ARGS=("$@")
